title: accent-color
description: Sets the color used by the browser's native form controls — checkboxes, radio buttons, range sliders, and progress bars. One property replaces complex custom control CSS. Baseline widely available since 2022.
html:
<form class="form">
  <label class="field"><input type="checkbox" checked> Checkbox checked</label>
  <label class="field"><input type="checkbox"> Checkbox unchecked</label>
  <label class="field"><input type="radio" name="r" checked> Radio selected</label>
  <label class="field"><input type="radio" name="r"> Radio unselected</label>
  <label class="field">Range: <input type="range" value="65"></label>
  <label class="field">Progress: <progress value="65" max="100"></progress></label>
</form>
challenge: All controls use the default browser blue. Set a single property on .form to change every control's accent to purple (#a855f7) — no custom checkbox CSS needed.
realWorldUsage: Design systems use accent-color on a form wrapper or :root to apply brand color to all native inputs at once, avoiding the need for fully custom controls that must replicate focus, keyboard, and accessibility behavior from scratch.
